jokes.text: No! No! Bad dog! Let go!
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper is causing chaos, presumably destroying something
jokes.punchline: Homer's escalating desperation and loss of control
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy opener - Homer's frustrated tone sets up the episode's central conflict with the dog

jokes.text: Bad Santa's Little Helper! Stop! Not the sports section!
jokes.setup: Homer addressing the dog by full name shows his distress
jokes.punchline: His specific concern about the sports section reveals his priorities
jokes.explanation: Good character specificity - Homer caring most about the sports section is perfectly in character

jokes.text: No! Stay away! This is not for you! This is Homer food!
jokes.setup: Homer protecting his food from the dog
jokes.punchline: Referring to it as 'Homer food' in third person like talking to a toddler
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike speech pattern and food obsession perfectly captured

jokes.text: I'll make it, Mom. Just tape my lunch box to my hand.
jokes.setup: Lisa claims she can make it to school despite being sick
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of taping a lunch box to her hand shows how weak she really is
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual absurdity with perfect economy - the tape detail is both specific and ridiculous

jokes.text: Yes, master.
jokes.setup: Marge tells Homer to feed the dog since it's hungry
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic, overly formal submission
jokes.explanation: Standard marital dynamic comedy - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Not that I'm angry, but how did you get my home number?
jokes.setup: Marge calls Dr. Hibbert at home
jokes.punchline: His passive-aggressive response claiming he's not angry while clearly being annoyed
jokes.explanation: Mild professional comedy - Dr. Hibbert's polite irritation is believable

jokes.text: Well, Mrs. Simpson... I'd like to rely on your diagnosis, but it'd be derelict of me to do so.
jokes.setup: Marge has diagnosed Lisa with mumps
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert's polite way of saying he can't trust a housewife's medical opinion
jokes.explanation: Good professional satire - the formal language makes the dismissal funnier

jokes.text: No way, she's faking. If Lisa stays home, I stay home.
jokes.setup: Family discussing Lisa's illness
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately sees an opportunity to skip school
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart behavior - opportunistic and school-averse

jokes.text: If Bart stays home, I go to school.
jokes.setup: Bart wants to stay home if Lisa does
jokes.punchline: Lisa would rather go to school sick than stay home with Bart
jokes.explanation: Excellent sibling dynamic - Lisa's academic dedication vs. her dislike of Bart

jokes.text: Wait. If Lisa goes to school, I go. But if she stays, I stay, so... Lisa goes to school...
jokes.setup: Bart trying to work out the logic of the school situation
jokes.punchline: His convoluted reasoning that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic comedy - Bart's attempt at reasoning is appropriately flawed

jokes.text: Don't confuse him.
jokes.setup: Bart's confusing logical loop
jokes.punchline: Marge's protective but resigned comment about Homer's mental capacity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - protective of Homer while acknowledging his limitations

jokes.text: Lisa, you wasted chicken pox. Don't waste the mumps.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to do homework while sick
jokes.punchline: Bart's perspective that illnesses are opportunities to be 'wasted'
jokes.explanation: Excellent kid perspective joke - the idea of 'wasting' an illness is both absurd and relatable

jokes.text: Bart-dude! Hey, Otto-man! Yo, hairy bro.
jokes.setup: Bart greeting Otto the bus driver
jokes.punchline: The casual, surfer-dude linguistic style between them
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Otto's laid-back hippie personality

jokes.text: Wandering mongrel. Get out of my mom-and-pop operation.
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper in the Kwik-E-Mart
jokes.punchline: Apu's formal language and the literal description of his store type
jokes.explanation: Apu's overly formal speech pattern is character-appropriate

jokes.text: Your canine is in my pool again. I'm calling the dog warden.
jokes.setup: Neighbor calling about the dog
jokes.punchline: The formal 'canine' language and immediate threat escalation
jokes.explanation: Standard neighbor complaint comedy with formal language twist

jokes.text: You go ahead and call your precious dog warden, you old axe... because my dog is tied up!
jokes.setup: Homer defending against the neighbor's complaint
jokes.punchline: His aggressive response followed by confident assertion that proves wrong
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the visual reveal - Homer's misplaced confidence

jokes.text: I am looking at him right--
jokes.setup: Homer confidently stating his dog is tied up
jokes.punchline: The cutoff implies he sees the empty leash/rope
jokes.explanation: Classic visual comedy beat - the interrupted sentence tells the whole story

jokes.text: Well, how-doodlie-do. Getting some exercise? Good for you.
jokes.setup: Ned encountering Homer during his exercise
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic overly cheerful greeting with made-up word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned characterization - the invented 'doodlie-do' is signature Flanders

jokes.text: Look at this rascal. He's a wet boy, isn't he? You're a water monster.
jokes.setup: Ned finding the wet dog
jokes.punchline: His childlike, overly affectionate way of talking to the dog
jokes.explanation: Good Ned character work - his innocent, childlike interaction with pets

jokes.text: Well, well, 'Mr. Universe' takes a walk.
jokes.setup: Homer exercising in elaborate gear
jokes.punchline: Ned's gentle mocking of Homer's appearance/fitness level
jokes.explanation: Good neighbor ribbing - Ned's polite way of poking fun at Homer

jokes.text: 'Mr. Universe,' I wish.
jokes.setup: Ned's sarcastic 'Mr. Universe' comment
jokes.punchline: Homer's self-aware response acknowledging his poor fitness
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Homer self-awareness - acknowledges his physical limitations

jokes.text: They've got Velcro, a water pump, built-in odometer, and vanity plates.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his expensive running shoes
jokes.punchline: The escalating list of ridiculous features, ending with vanity plates
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - vanity plates on shoes is perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: My heart rate's dropping. Better skedaddle-y-do.
jokes.setup: Ned ending the conversation to continue exercising
jokes.punchline: Another made-up Ned word combining 'skedaddle' and his 'diddly' speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Another great Ned neologism - the wordplay is character-specific and memorable

jokes.text: Before you know it, you'll be back among your polygons... and your Euclidian algorithms.
jokes.setup: Lisa says her favorite subject is arithmetic
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert's overly sophisticated description of basic math
jokes.explanation: Good professional pretension - Dr. Hibbert overcomplicate elementary concepts

jokes.text: Oh, the kissing disease.
jokes.setup: Lisa has mumps
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate association/misunderstanding of the disease
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding - confusing mumps with mono

jokes.text: My little girl is growing up.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks mumps are sexually transmitted
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate proud father moment
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable parent moment - Homer's misguided pride in his daughter's imagined maturity

jokes.text: Teen Dream, Teen Scream... and Teen Steam magazine?
jokes.setup: Lisa requesting magazines while sick
jokes.punchline: The escalating names of increasingly questionable teen magazines
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity - 'Teen Steam' implies increasingly inappropriate content

jokes.text: It smells historic.
jokes.setup: Marge showing Lisa the family quilt
jokes.punchline: Lisa's childlike but perceptive description of age
jokes.explanation: Charming kid perspective - 'smells historic' is both funny and accurate

jokes.text: The memory of a million drop stitches flows in your veins.
jokes.setup: Marge teaching Lisa to sew
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of inherited sewing failures
jokes.explanation: Great Marge moment - treating domestic failure as noble heritage

jokes.text: Now that's a sewing finger, honey.
jokes.setup: Teaching Lisa to sew by developing callouses
jokes.punchline: Marge's pride in Lisa's developing work-hardened finger
jokes.explanation: Good domestic comedy - taking pride in manual labor markers

jokes.text: They're for my daughter. Sure. Wise guy.
jokes.setup: Homer buying teen magazines
jokes.punchline: The clerk's obvious disbelief and Homer's awareness of the judgment
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment - the universal awkwardness of buying embarrassing items

jokes.text: Simpson! I order you to buy those shoes! You're the boss!
jokes.setup: Homer hesitating about the expensive shoes
jokes.punchline: Burns appearing to order Homer to make a frivolous purchase
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist moment - Burns randomly enabling Homer's bad spending

jokes.text: 'Keep On Truckin'?' What does that mean? I didn't know then. I don't know now.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at his old shoes with the slogan
jokes.punchline: His honest confusion about the dated expression across time
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy about meaningless cultural phrases and the passage of time

jokes.text: How many of these guys are Corey? Eight.
jokes.setup: Homer delivering teen magazines to Lisa
jokes.punchline: The prevalence of teen heartthrobs named Corey in the late 80s
jokes.explanation: Great cultural observation about the 'Two Coreys' era of teen magazines

jokes.text: Phonics, functions, vocabulary, remedial reading? Do your own homework, Bart!
jokes.setup: Bart bringing Lisa her homework
jokes.punchline: Lisa realizing it's actually Bart's remedial work
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff showing Bart trying to get Lisa to do his remedial work.

jokes.text: You bought those smoke alarms, and we haven't had a single fire.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his purchase against Marge's complaint
jokes.punchline: His backwards logic that successful fire prevention proves the alarms were unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - completely backwards reasoning about cause and effect

jokes.text: 'Satan's Little Helper.' Santa's Little Helper.
jokes.setup: Instructor misreading the dog's name
jokes.punchline: The humorous misunderstanding that makes the dog sound demonic
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay - Satan/Santa confusion creates comedy through menacing misinterpretation

jokes.text: Nonsense taught by charlatans and learned by bloody twits!
jokes.setup: Instructor dismissing conventional dog training wisdom
jokes.punchline: Her harsh, dramatic condemnation using formal language
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - the instructor's extreme, formal hostility

jokes.text: I'll tell you the most important words you'll ever hear... choke chain.
jokes.setup: Dramatic buildup about important wisdom
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic and somewhat harsh 'choke chain' as the profound truth
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimactic punchline - buildup to mundane/harsh dog training advice

jokes.text: The dog's eyes will cross, and his tongue will protrude slightly.
jokes.setup: Explaining how to know you're training correctly
jokes.punchline: Description that sounds like signs of strangulation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about overly harsh training methods described clinically

jokes.text: Is my dog dead, ma'am? You don't know how often I'm asked that.
jokes.setup: Student concerned about their dog's condition
jokes.punchline: Instructor's casual admission that this is a frequent concern
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - the casual acknowledgment of near-death training incidents

jokes.text: I don't know. I just dip in and out. I'm only watching because Brandy knows the body is hidden in the boathouse.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if soap operas are always this dramatic
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual knowledge of complex soap opera plots despite claiming not to watch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - denying investment while demonstrating deep soap opera knowledge

jokes.text: The other, like a hose without a fireman. Which way do you think that was? Like a hose, you wrinkled queen.
jokes.setup: Instructor asking Homer to evaluate how his dog urinated
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude but accurate assessment and the instructor's harsh insult
jokes.explanation: Good crude humor with character-appropriate insults from the harsh instructor

jokes.text: My dog was bringing me my shoes, and they fell apart in his mouth. Our warranty doesn't cover fire, theft or acts of dog.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to return his expensive shoes that fell apart
jokes.punchline: The store's oddly specific warranty exclusion for 'acts of dog'
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist legal language - 'acts of dog' parallel to 'acts of God' is brilliant

jokes.text: If you'd like to buy, they're $1. So that's your little plan! Get us addicted, then jack up the price!
jokes.setup: Free samples of macadamia nuts
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid conspiracy theory about the one-dollar price
jokes.explanation: Great Homer paranoia - seeing conspiracy in completely reasonable business practices

jokes.text: Well, you win.
jokes.setup: Homer accusing vendor of having a conspiracy
jokes.punchline: His immediate capitulation and purchase despite his objections
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - acknowledging defeat while still buying the product

jokes.text: He taught that the noblest concerto can be drained of its beauty.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing her music teacher Mr. Largo
jokes.punchline: Her sophisticated but damning assessment of bad teaching
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa wisdom - sophisticated criticism of poor education

jokes.text: He taught me that music is a fire that comes out your mouth. So you better stick an instrument in front of it.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing Bleeding Gums Murphy's influence
jokes.punchline: The vivid, slightly absurd metaphor for musical expression
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Lisa moment - poetic and funny description of musical passion

jokes.text: My quilt! Six generations, ruined!
jokes.setup: Discovering the family quilt has been damaged
jokes.punchline: Marge's dramatic reaction to the loss of family heritage
jokes.explanation: Standard domestic crisis - Marge's attachment to family traditions

jokes.text: My cookie!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to console Marge about the quilt
jokes.punchline: His immediate shift to personal outrage about his own loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness - abandoning empathy the moment his own interests are threatened

jokes.text: We never did this before. We never had a family member we can give away.
jokes.setup: Family meeting about the dog's behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's implication that they would give away family members if they could
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer humor - casual consideration of disposing of family members

jokes.text: I'll set fire to my hair, rip my clothes!
jokes.setup: Bart protesting giving away the dog
jokes.punchline: His dramatic threat of self-harm as protest
jokes.explanation: Standard kid tantrum escalation - appropriate dramatic overreaction

jokes.text: I've suffered at the paws of this dog... but when I look into his vacant eyes, I can't bear to let him go.
jokes.setup: Bart's emotional defense of the dog
jokes.punchline: The play on 'hands/paws' and the backhanded description of 'vacant eyes'
jokes.explanation: Great Bart moment - mixing genuine emotion with accurate but insulting description

jokes.text: We can question his disposition, but we can't question his heart.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending the dog to the family
jokes.punchline: Her mature, philosophical defense of pet loyalty
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa wisdom - sophisticated emotional defense in simple terms

jokes.text: If they're ever going to pull the plug on me, I want you in my corner.
jokes.setup: Lisa's passionate defense of the dog
jokes.punchline: Homer's morbid but practical assessment of her advocacy skills
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer dark humor - practical consideration of end-of-life scenarios

jokes.text: You moldy old maid.
jokes.setup: Training instructor giving Homer a toffee for success
jokes.punchline: Homer's ungrateful insult after receiving praise
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer rudeness - inappropriate response to kindness

jokes.text: Free to loving home, world's smartest dog. Says 'I love you' on command.
jokes.setup: Writing an ad for the dog before he's failed his test
jokes.punchline: The ironic claims about the dog's intelligence and abilities
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup - false advertising for a clearly problematic dog

jokes.text: Take a walk. Sniff that other dog's butt. See? He does exactly what I say.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to demonstrate the dog's obedience to a potential buyer
jokes.punchline: Claiming credit for natural dog behaviors as obedience
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - taking credit for instinctive animal behavior

jokes.text: He'd be happy at my farm. People think only mules can pull carts. Impatient people think that, patient people know better.
jokes.setup: Farmer wanting to adopt the dog
jokes.punchline: His plan to use the dog as farm labor with philosophical justification
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about animal exploitation disguised as wisdom

jokes.text: Heavens to murgatroid. Perhaps I cling to the old ways like a well-chewed shoe...
jokes.setup: Homer asking instructor to just let his dog pass
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic, overly elaborate rejection with dog-related metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - overly dramatic speech with perfect dog-training metaphor

jokes.text: We do not need another college graduate who doesn't know how to sit!
jokes.setup: Instructor's passionate speech about standards
jokes.punchline: Her comparison of poorly trained dogs to ineffective college graduates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary - comparing dog training to educational standards

jokes.text: I'm sorry. You can't help being dumb.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to train his dog
jokes.punchline: His blunt, inappropriate consolation to the animal
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer insensitivity - inappropriate honesty toward his pet

jokes.text: This patch commemorates the destruction of the old quilt.
jokes.setup: Marge making a new quilt patch
jokes.punchline: Her decision to commemorate the disaster rather than ignore it
jokes.explanation: Good Marge wisdom - finding constructive meaning in domestic disasters

jokes.text: If you knew how to learn-- Sit. Wait a minute, you did it!
jokes.setup: Bart giving up on the dog's ability to learn
jokes.punchline: The dog finally succeeding just as Bart expresses hopelessness
jokes.explanation: Good timing comedy - success arriving at the moment of giving up

jokes.text: You son of a bitch! Good show!
jokes.setup: Bart celebrating the dog's successful training
jokes.punchline: The literal accuracy of 'son of a bitch' followed by British praise
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay - literal truth of the insult plus the formal praise creates perfect comedy
